candidates.why: Explicit total revenue line representing the company’s aggregate top-line; values align with scale of the business.
Also a revenue concept but appears unused (nan) while Total Revenue is populated, so less appropriate.
Represents a component of revenue rather than the consolidated top line.
Balancing/plug line, not a clean economic revenue measure.
Explicitly a balancing item, not appropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Total Revenue clearly serves as consolidated top-line in this statement.

candidates.why: Direct costs tied to providing services/projects, typical COGS component for a contracting/service business.
Direct material costs, a classic COGS component.
Balance sheet stock, not a period expense; unsuitable for COGS.
Balance sheet inventory, not an income statement cost flow line.

variables.notes: Both Subcontractors and Cost of materials are direct production/service costs and together represent COGS.

candidates.why: Aggregated operating overhead bucket (excludes clearly identified COGS lines); appropriate when detailed SG&A components are not fully broken out.
Subcomponent of operating expenses; including it together with Operating Expenses would double count part of the same bucket.
Explicit SG&A line, which should be preferred per hierarchy even if preview values are nan.

variables.notes: Following rule (b): explicit SG&A row exists but appears empty in preview, so include it plus the broad ‘Operating Expenses’ bucket to capture SG&A across all years. Exclude ‘Other Operating Expenses’ to avoid double-counting a subset of Operating Expenses.
No R&D-related candidate rows were provided; treat XRD as zero.

candidates.why: Primary line for interest expense; core component of XINT.
Additional interest expense component distinct from main Interest Expenses line.
Interest related to lease liabilities; economically an interest expense and should be included in XINT.
Represents profit attributable to minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests, not an interest expense.
Supplemental line with no populated values; main Interest Expenses row already captures interest cost.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplementary disclosure of lease interest; including it alongside ‘Interest on Financial Lease’ risks double-counting the same economic cost.
Balance sheet liability, not a period interest expense.
Specifically non-interest bearing; not interest expense.
Equity attributable to minorities, not interest expense.
Equity attributable to minorities, not interest expense.
Represents future contractual interest within lease disclosures, not the actual period expense line in the income statement.

variables.notes: Selected all distinct income-statement interest expense components (including lease interest) while excluding supplemental/duplicative and non-P&L items.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent
candidates.why: Represents common shareholders’ equity; however excludes non-controlling interests whereas BE definition allows total equity as well.
Not populated and too narrow; not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to sharholders of the parent
candidates.why: Same concept as prior line, with slightly different spelling; appears to be the main parent equity figure across all years.
Includes both parent and non-controlling interests and matches the stated definition allowing total equity/book equity.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to sharholders of the parent - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing/plug line, not appropriate as BE.
Explicit balancing item; not suitable for BE.
Balancing item; not actual equity amount.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row, not the equity figure itself.
Represents total assets/liabilities; not equity alone.

variables.notes: Using Total equity as book equity, consistent with allowance for total equity in BE definition.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to minority interests, not the balance-sheet MIB stock; still valid conceptually but balance-sheet figure is preferred for BE-level minority interest.
Duplicate label of non-controlling interests; same issue as above.
OCI currency translation for minorities; not the minority interest balance itself.
Refers to minority share of comprehensive income, not the equity balance.
Balance sheet carrying amount of non-controlling (minority) interests; best match for MIB.
Alternative label for the same concept; including both would double count MIB.

variables.notes: Chose the balance sheet ‘Non-controlling interests’ as the minority interest (MIB) stock; did not include the duplicate ‘Minority Interest’ row to avoid double counting.
